newIncidents.title: FCC chair threatens broadcast licenses after Trump criticizes Iran war coverage
newIncidents.description: The FCC chair directly threatened to revoke broadcast licenses of news networks following President Trump's complaints about their coverage of the Iran conflict, representing a significant threat to press freedom through regulatory weaponization. Source

newIncidents.title: Judge finds DOJ weaponized subpoenas against Fed chair Powell
newIncidents.description: A federal judge issued a scathing ruling that the Trump administration weaponized DOJ subpoenas to pressure Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell, tossing the subpoenas and finding significant separation of powers violations. Source

newIncidents.title: Trump refuses to sign any bills until voting restriction legislation passes
newIncidents.description: President Trump announced he will not sign any legislation into law until Congress passes the SAVE America Act, using executive veto power as leverage to force passage of voting restrictions and demanding elimination of the filibuster. Source

newIncidents.title: Federal judge rules three DOJ officials unlawfully serving in New Jersey
newIncidents.description: For the second time, a federal judge ruled that top DOJ officials in New Jersey are serving unlawfully while circumventing Senate confirmation requirements. Source

newIncidents.title: Trump administration widens 2020 election probe with Arizona subpoenas
newIncidents.description: The DOJ issued grand jury subpoenas for Arizona state legislative records as part of an expanding investigation into the 2020 election, raising concerns about politicized use of investigative powers. Source

newCheckEvents.text: FCC chair threatens to revoke broadcast licenses of news networks after Trump complains about Iran war coverage. Source
Federal judge rules Trump administration weaponized DOJ subpoenas against Federal Reserve chair Powell in scathing opinion. Source
Trump announces he will not sign any bills until Congress passes voting restriction legislation, demanding elimination of filibuster. Source
Federal judge rules for second time that top DOJ officials in New Jersey are serving unlawfully while circumventing Senate confirmation. Source

newTickerItems.label: ELECTIONS
newTickerItems.text: DOJ expands 2020 election probe with Arizona grand jury subpoenas

updates.summary: Multiple significant developments show continued institutional strain: FCC chair threatened broadcast licenses following Trump's criticism of Iran war coverage; federal judge ruled DOJ weaponized subpoenas against Fed chair Powell; Trump announced he will block all legislation until voting restrictions pass; and DOJ expanded 2020 election investigations with Arizona subpoenas. Judicial rulings continue to find Trump DOJ officials serving unlawfully.
